\[ x' = x\cos(60^\circ) - y\sin(60^\circ) \]
\[ y' = x\sin(60^\circ) + y\cos(60^\circ) \]
where $(x', y')$ is the new coordinate after rotation. The original parabola is $y = x^2 - 4$. I will substitute the rotation formulas into the original equation to get the equation of the rotated parabola. Then, I will solve for the intersection points.

# Substitute the rotation formulas into the original equation

rotated_eq = rotated_eq.simplify()

# Solve for the intersection points
